title: Canopy wayfinding and cavern loop phase
summary: Thirty-eighth structured packet for Eco Explorer. This is a future lane-3 follow-on after the current canopy-and-cavern growth wave: add one upper-canopy continuation and one calmer cavern-loop follow-up, using tiny wayfinding and recovery cues so the vertical-exploration family feels more coherent without turning into a maze or platform gauntlet.

execution_order.why_now: After the current vertical family wave, the canopy is the clearest next place to extend.
This is the fastest way to make the giant-tree branch feel more complete.
Height and readability need an early check before the lane deepens further.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the cavern loop and wayfinding follow-up.
execution_order.why_now: Once the canopy side is stronger, the cave side should get a clearer return rhythm.

execution_order.goal: Implement the cavern loop and wayfinding follow-up.
execution_order.why_now: This is the smallest follow-on that makes the deeper cave family feel more coherent.

execution_order.goal: Review the canopy and cavern loop follow-on.
execution_order.why_now: Lane 3 should only keep growing if the vertical family stays readable and inviting.

guardrails: Do not add combat, danger meters, or a larger traversal HUD.
acceptance_focus: The canopy and cave branches feel more coherent as one vertical family.
Lane 3 keeps growing through place identity rather than difficulty spikes.
implementation_notes: For `main-119`, prefer one tiny trunk-interior or bark-window continuation inside the existing old-growth canopy family rather than another broad height spike.
Keep the current top route (`main trunk -> upper snag -> canopy rung -> high perch`) as the only approach, then add one short new climbable and one compact upper shelf if needed.
Do not add a second vertical cue or denser marker spread; rely on existing trunk visibility and the current canopy-opening cue.
If visual support is needed, slightly widen the existing `old-growth-canopy-pocket` and `old-growth-trunk-interior` instead of inventing a new depth-feature style.

For `main-120`, keep `stone-basin` as the deepest point and spend the budget on one same-footprint upper-return nook or shelf tied to the current `root-hollow-cave-trunk`, not on a wider tunnel or another lower chamber.
Reuse the current cave cue language if the loop needs support; at most move or add one more tiny `recovery-light`-style cave cue rather than inventing a second marker type.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-3.md, docs/reports/2026-03-30-canopy-wayfinding-and-cavern-loop-phase.md, docs/reports/2026-03-30-upper-canopy-continuation-review.md, docs/reports/2026-03-30-cavern-loop-follow-up-handoff.md, and this packet. Treat packet `038` as a two-part lane-3 follow-on: the canopy half is now closed, so the next live step should strengthen the cave side through one same-footprint upper-return loop plus tiny reused cave wayfinding, keeping the vertical family coherent without becoming harsher.
